title: Accelerating Sales with AWS — Knowledge Check

questions.label: Question 1: Navigating Internal Motivations
questions.scenario: Understanding your counterpart's financial targets is critical. Across AWS personas — Account Manager (AM), Partner Sales Manager (PSM), and Partner Development Manager (PDM) — select the one Goal Badge that accurately reflects the Partner Sales Manager's specific financial motivation.

options.label: A
options.text: Increase Direct AWS Spend

options.label: B
options.text: Drive Marketplace Purchases

options.label: C
options.text: Grow Partner-Allocated Spend

options.label: D
options.text: Improve Regional Partnership KPIs

questions.label: Question 2: Pitching the Account Manager
questions.scenario: You are on a virtual call with an AWS Account Manager who says, "AppsFlyer is a SaaS solution; it won't immediately expand my customer's cloud compute spend today. Why should I help you?" Select the one effective pitch to win their advocacy.

options.label: A
options.text: We offer a trusted entry point into protected marketing budgets.

options.label: B
options.text: As our platform scales their marketing, their overall AWS infrastructure will organically scale.

options.label: C
options.text: Our marketplace revenue is growing 4x YoY.

options.label: D
options.text: We offer massive AWS compute discounts.

questions.label: Question 3: Dismantling Bureaucracy
questions.scenario: Your deal is stuck. The customer's IT and Finance departments are blocking the purchase due to budget constraints. Select the one correct mechanism that Marketplace co-selling enables to fundamentally overcome these budget constraints by reducing the "Buyer Cost-Sensitivity" meter.

options.label: A
options.text: Retire up to 25% of Enterprise Discount Plan (EDP) commitments via Marketplace purchases.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow the customer to bypass procurement review by utilizing pre-approved AWS credits.

options.label: C
options.text: Utilize the customer's unspent AWS committed spend (EDP/CPO) before year-end expiration.

options.label: D
options.text: Convert a portion of AppsFlyer's SaaS purchase into the customer's AWS spending commitment.

questions.type: Exposing Foundational Assumptions and Contradicting Knowledge
questions.label: Question 4: Exposing Dangerous Assumptions
questions.scenario: Read the statement: "AWS Account Managers expect us to resell their cloud infrastructure." Select the one category that applies to this statement.

options.label: A
options.text: Co-Selling Advantage

options.label: B
options.text: Dangerous Misconception

options.label: C
options.text: Relating to Marketplace

options.label: D
options.text: Financial Strategy

questions.label: Question 5: Flawless Execution
questions.scenario: You have just identified a massive Tier-1 enterprise account where AWS co-selling will provide a definitive, deal-winning edge. Select the one action that represents the most critical, immediate, and high-impact step to secure AWS support.

options.label: A
options.text: Open an internal ad-hoc request ASAP.

options.label: B
options.text: Proactively reach out to your Partner Development Manager (PDM) to establish momentum.

options.label: C
options.text: Utilize the specific outreach templates and playbooks on the AppsFlyer AWS Hub.

options.label: D
options.text: Pitch the AWS rep on the deal mechanics since they aren't marketing-savvy.
